sections.paragraph:
(like the kindred verbs זָהַר, טָהַר, צָהַל, see under צָחַח ), to shine. Arab. ظهر to appear, to come forth, to reveal oneself, and طهر to be pure; both coming from the idea of shining. Hence צֹהַר light, splendour, and יִצְהָר oil, so called from its brightness.

Hiphil (denom. from יִצְהָר), to squeeze out oil (in a press), Job 24:11.
